title: Operation Rheinübung — The Bismarck Sortie
summary: Operation Rheinübung was the sortie of the battleship Bismarck and heavy cruiser Prinz Eugen into the Atlantic to attack Allied convoys. The operation ended with the sinking of HMS Hood and the destruction of the Bismarck nine days after she sailed.

key_events.event: Battle of the Denmark Strait — Bismarck sinks HMS Hood in under 10 minutes; 1,415 killed, 3 survivors. HMS Prince of Wales damaged.
Royal Navy hunts Bismarck across the Atlantic. Bismarck shakes pursuit but sends a long radio message that allows triangulation of her position.
Swordfish torpedo aircraft from HMS Ark Royal hit Bismarck's rudder, leaving her unable to manoeuvre.
HMS King George V and HMS Rodney shell Bismarck into a burning wreck. She sinks; 2,086 killed, 115 survivors.

outcome: Allied victory. Bismarck destroyed; Prinz Eugen escaped to Brest. The operation ended German hopes of disrupting Atlantic supply lines with surface raiders.
